motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast and still, 26°F all morning with no intention of improving. A low ceiling of cloud the colour of old pewter sits over the valley without moving. No wind. The lake is flat under the ice. The geese are where the geese always are in November, which is to say underfoot.

undercurrent.note: The season has closed and the town is ordinary in all the ways it knows how to be ordinary. The level drops to 8 — the accommodation is complete, the hum is memory, the returned are simply home. The new thread is barely a thread: Calloway has mentioned the east alcove in passing, has used the word 'acquisitions,' has noted it has been sitting empty long enough. This is true. It has been sitting empty long enough. The shelving unit has been sourced. Nobody is afraid of new shelves. The phenomenon has not announced itself yet and will not for some days; it is patient, and the books are patient, and the alcove has had fourteen years of practice at waiting. Stage holds at 1 because the town is calm but the alcove is in the record now, and things that get into the record have a way of becoming the next thing the town has to accommodate.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: The library's east alcove has been empty since 2009, when the last cataloguer left in the middle of a Wednesday with no explanation. Calloway has begun shelving it. The books that appear on the new shelves overnight are not books the library owns, and they are not books that have ever been published, but they are about Greywater Falls — and they are set in years that have not happened yet.
